report_name: Russian APT Turla Unleashes New 'STOCKSTAY' Backdoor in Ukraine Espionage Attacks

threat_actors.name: Turla
threat_actors.description: Highly sophisticated Russian state-sponsored group observed deploying the STOCKSTAY backdoor in cyber espionage campaigns against government and military organizations in Ukraine and entities related to Italian foreign policy.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The STOCKSTAY backdoor communicates with its C2 servers using a secure WebSocket connection to provide a persistent, full-duplex communication channel.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-hop Proxy
attack_patterns.description: Turla employs a multi-hop C2 infrastructure, including a public GitHub repository acting as a controller, to proxy traffic and hide the true location of its final C2 servers.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Turla has been observed using compromised WordPress sites for operational purposes for both STOCKSTAY and Kazuar malware families.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Turla uses encoded PowerShell commands for initial execution or lateral movement, a common TTP for the group.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The STOCKSTAY backdoor is deployed onto target systems, transferred from actor-controlled infrastructure.

malware.name: STOCKSTAY
malware.description: A new, sophisticated .NET backdoor written using the Windows Forms framework. It establishes persistent access, executes commands, and exfiltrates data from compromised Windows systems, communicating over secure WebSockets.

malware.name: Kazuar
malware.description: A long-standing .NET implant in Turla's arsenal. STOCKSTAY shares significant code overlap with Kazuar and is considered its successor. It has been observed using compromised WordPress sites for C2.

identities.name: Government and military entities in Ukraine
identities.description: Primary targets of Turla's cyber espionage campaign using the STOCKSTAY backdoor.

identities.name: Organizations related to Italian foreign policy
identities.description: Secondary targets of Turla's cyber espionage campaign using the STOCKSTAY backdoor.

infrastructure.name: Turla Multi-hop C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: A multi-hop C2 infrastructure used by Turla to hide the true location of its servers and relay commands to the STOCKSTAY backdoor.

infrastructure.name: GitHub C2 Controller
infrastructure.description: A public GitHub repository containing a Python script that acts as a victim-side WebSocket server controller, part of Turla's multi-hop C2 infrastructure.

indicators.name: Anomalous Outbound WebSocket Connection
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Outbound WebSocket Connection
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4a7b-8c9d-e0f1a2b3c4d5
status: experimental
description: Detects outbound WebSocket (wss:// or ws://) connections from non-browser processes, which could indicate C2 communication from implants like STOCKSTAY.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/google-details-turlas-new-stockstay.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|startswith: 
      - 'wss://'
      - 'ws://'
  filter_main_browsers:
    c-useragent|contains:
      - 'Mozilla/5.0'
  condition: selection and not filter_main_browsers
indicators.description: Detects outbound WebSocket connections from non-browser processes, a behavior associated with the STOCKSTAY backdoor's C2 communication.

indicators.name: Potential Turla Payload Execution via Rundll32
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Turla Payload Execution via Rundll32
id: e2b3c4d5-f6a7-4b8c-9d0e-f1a2b3c4d5e6
status: experimental
description: Monitors for rundll32.exe spawning unusual child processes or loading .NET assemblies, a TTP associated with Turla.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/google-details-turlas-new-stockstay.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\werfault.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects process creation events where the parent is rundll32.exe, a known TTP for Turla to launch its .NET payloads.

indicators.name: Suspicious Encoded PowerShell Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Encoded PowerShell Command
id: d3c4e5f6-a7b8-4c9d-0e1f-2a3b4c5d6e7f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of encoded PowerShell commands, a common technique used by APTs like Turla for execution and obfuscation.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/google-details-turlas-new-stockstay.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
      - ' -e '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with encoded command arguments, a technique frequently used by Turla.

indicators.name: Potential STOCKSTAY Payload Drop
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential STOCKSTAY Payload Drop
id: c4d5e6f7-b8c9-4d0e-1f2a-3b4c5d6e7f8a
status: experimental
description: Monitors for newly created .NET DLLs in temporary or user-profile directories, a potential indicator of STOCKSTAY payload deployment.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/google-details-turlas-new-stockstay.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-26
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.dll'
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\Users\'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of new DLL files in user temporary directories, a potential sign of the STOCKSTAY payload being dropped.

relationships.target_name: Government and military entities in Ukraine
Organizations related to Italian foreign policy

relationships.source_name: Potential Turla Payload Execution via Rundll32
